# STM32L5 TrustZone (Secure/Non‑Secure) — CLion/CMake Project, generated by STM32CubeMX

### Tools:
 * CLion2026.1+
 * STM32CubeMX 6.16.0
 * STM32CubeCLT 1.19.0
 * Nucleo-L552ZE-Q

This repository contains a minimal STM32L552 (Cortex‑M33 with TrustZone‑M) example split into Secure (S) and Non‑Secure (NS) applications, configured for CLion and CMake.

### Highlights
- TrustZone split: separate CMake targets for Secure and Non‑Secure worlds.
- Ready‑to‑use CLion CMake presets and targets.

### Project layout
- Secure/ — Secure world app
  - Core/Inc, Core/Src — application, IRQs, HAL init, secure veneers (`secure_nsc.c`)
  - Core/Startup — startup assembly for STM32L552 (Secure)
  - *.ld — Secure linker scripts
- NonSecure/ — Non‑Secure world app
- CMakeLists.txt, CMakePresets.json — top‑level CMake

## Build (with CLion)
1) Open the project in CLion.
2) Choose the active profile (e.g., Debug) configured by the workspace.
3) Build `Secure+NonSecure` target



### Debug server (ST‑LINK GDB Server — Generic ST‑Link S+NS)
- Probe: onboard ST‑LINK/V3 on the Nucleo‑L552ZE‑Q.
- Server: ST‑LINK GDB Server, shipped with STM32CubeCLT or STM32CubeIDE.

#### What it is
- A single ST‑LINK GDB Server configuration that program/debug both Secure (S) and Non‑Secure (NS) images within one session.
- The server halts after reset in Secure state and allows breakpoints/stepping when execution crosses S↔NS via NSC veneers.

#### Custom connection script
```txt
echo Connecting...\n
$GDBTargetCommand$
echo Connected!!\n

echo *********************************\n
echo Loading NonSecure file...\n
exec-file NonSecure/build/stm32l5-trustzone_NS.elf
echo Flashing...
load

echo *********************************\n
echo Loading Secure file...\n
file Secure/build/stm32l5-trustzone_S.elf
echo Flashing...\n
load

echo *********************************\n
echo Loading NonSecure symbols file...\n
add-symbol-file NonSecure/build/stm32l5-trustzone_NS.elf

echo *********************************\n
echo Reset\n
monitor reset
```

### Notes
- Always keep S and NS images consistent; if you update one, ensure vectors and TrustZone setup remain compatible.
- ST’s HAL/CMSIS code retains its original licenses under `Drivers/**/LICENSE*`.
- Project scaffolding is provided for educational/testing purposes.
